title: General Swimming Safety Guidelines
description: Essential safety rules for all swimming activities
guidelines: Never swim alone in open water
Always check weather conditions before swimming
Stay hydrated and take regular breaks
Know your limits and don't exceed them
Learn proper swimming techniques before attempting advanced skills
Have a safety plan and emergency contacts available

title: Open Water Safety
description: Specific safety measures for swimming in lakes, oceans, and rivers
guidelines: Swim in designated areas with lifeguard supervision
Check water depth and current conditions
Avoid swimming during storms or rough weather
Be aware of marine life and potential hazards
Use bright swim caps and reflective gear for visibility
Keep a floatation device if you're not a strong swimmer
Inform someone of your swimming plans and expected return time

title: Pool Safety
description: Safety measures for swimming in pools and controlled environments
guidelines: Follow pool rules and posted regulations
Wait 30 minutes after eating before swimming
Use proper diving techniques in deep water
Be aware of pool depth changes and markings
Don't run on wet pool decks
Use sunscreen and reapply regularly
Shower before and after swimming to prevent skin irritation

title: Technique Safety
description: Safety measures related to proper swimming technique and injury prevention
guidelines: Learn proper breathing techniques to prevent hyperventilation
Warm up properly before intense swimming sessions
Use correct stroke techniques to prevent shoulder injuries
Don't push through pain - stop if something hurts
Practice proper flip turns to prevent head and neck injuries
Maintain proper body alignment to prevent back strain
Stay hydrated to prevent muscle cramps and fatigue
Take regular breaks to prevent overexertion

title: Health and Hygiene
description: Health guidelines for maintaining good swimming hygiene and preventing illness
guidelines: Shower before and after swimming to remove chlorine and bacteria
Wear swim cap to protect hair from pool chemicals
Use goggles to protect eyes from chlorine and irritation
Remove contact lenses before swimming to prevent loss or injury
Don't swallow pool water to avoid ingesting chemicals
Clean and dry ears after swimming to prevent infections
Use proper swimwear to prevent chafing and skin irritation
Stay hydrated to maintain optimal body function
Avoid swimming when sick to prevent spreading illness

title: Equipment Safety
description: Proper use and maintenance of swimming equipment for safety
guidelines: Use properly fitting goggles to prevent water leakage and eye irritation
Check swim caps for secure fit to prevent loss during swimming
Inspect kickboards and pull buoys for cracks or damage
Use appropriate swim fins for your skill level and pool conditions
Maintain proper spacing between swimmers to prevent equipment collisions
Store equipment properly to prevent damage and ensure longevity
Replace worn or damaged equipment immediately
Use sunscreen on exposed skin even when using equipment

title: Emergency Procedures
description: Emergency response procedures for swimming-related incidents
guidelines: Know location of emergency equipment and first aid kits
Learn basic rescue techniques and CPR if qualified
Have emergency contact numbers readily available
Signal for help immediately if in distress - use arm waves or whistle
Stay with the person in trouble until help arrives
Know how to recognize signs of drowning and distress
Practice emergency procedures regularly with swimming partners
Call emergency services (911) for serious incidents immediately
